([/diːˈkælb/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") [*dee-KALB*](/wiki/Help:Pronunciation_respelling_key "Help:Pronunciation respelling key"), [/ˈdiːkælb/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") [*DEE-kalb*](/wiki/Help:Pronunciation_respelling_key "Help:Pronunciation respelling key")) is a thoroughfare in the [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") [boroughs](/wiki/Borough_(New_York_City) "Borough (New York City)") of [Brooklyn](/wiki/Brooklyn "Brooklyn") and [Queens](/wiki/Queens "Queens"), with the majority of its length in Brooklyn.
["DeKalb Avenue Traffic Calming and Bicycle Lane Project"](http://nyc.gov/html/dot/downloads/pdf/dekalbave_cbpresentation.pdf) (PDF). NYCDOT. March 2008.

**Route map**:

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Avenue in Brooklyn, New York

[Template:Attached KML/DeKalb Avenue](/wiki/Template:Attached_KML/DeKalb_Avenue "Template:Attached KML/DeKalb Avenue")

KML is from Wikidata

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ec/DeKalb_Av_Ft_Green_Pk_jeh.JPG/250px-DeKalb_Av_Ft_Green_Pk_jeh.JPG)](/wiki/File:DeKalb_Av_Ft_Green_Pk_jeh.JPG)

At Fort Greene Park

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/cd/BPD_20th_Pct_Wilson_Kalb_jeh.JPG/250px-BPD_20th_Pct_Wilson_Kalb_jeh.JPG)](/wiki/File:BPD_20th_Pct_Wilson_Kalb_jeh.JPG)

At Wilson Avenue

**DeKalb Avenue** ([/diːˈkælb/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") [*dee-KALB*](/wiki/Help:Pronunciation_respelling_key "Help:Pronunciation respelling key"), [/ˈdiːkælb/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") [*DEE-kalb*](/wiki/Help:Pronunciation_respelling_key "Help:Pronunciation respelling key")) is a thoroughfare in the [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") [boroughs](/wiki/Borough_(New_York_City) "Borough (New York City)") of [Brooklyn](/wiki/Brooklyn "Brooklyn") and [Queens](/wiki/Queens "Queens"), with the majority of its length in Brooklyn.

It runs from Woodward Avenue (Linden Hill Cemetery) in [Ridgewood, Queens](/wiki/Ridgewood,_Queens "Ridgewood, Queens") to [Downtown Brooklyn](/wiki/Downtown_Brooklyn "Downtown Brooklyn"), terminating at the [Fulton Mall](/wiki/Fulton_Mall "Fulton Mall") where [the Brooklyn Tower](/wiki/The_Brooklyn_Tower "The Brooklyn Tower") and [City Point](/wiki/City_Point_(Brooklyn) "City Point (Brooklyn)") cross.

DeKalb Avenue is named after Baron [Johann de Kalb](/wiki/Johann_de_Kalb "Johann de Kalb"), who served in the [American Revolutionary War](/wiki/American_Revolutionary_War "American Revolutionary War").

## Notable buildings

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=DeKalb_Avenue&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Notable buildings")]

Landmarks along the avenue include the [Pratt Institute](/wiki/Pratt_Institute "Pratt Institute"), [Fort Greene Park](/wiki/Fort_Greene_Park "Fort Greene Park"), the [Brooklyn Hospital Center](/wiki/Brooklyn_Hospital_Center "Brooklyn Hospital Center"), the DeKalb and Marcy branches of the [Brooklyn Public Library](/wiki/Brooklyn_Public_Library "Brooklyn Public Library"), [Long Island University](/wiki/Long_Island_University "Long Island University")'s [Brooklyn Campus](/wiki/LIU_Brooklyn "LIU Brooklyn"), [Brooklyn Technical High School](/wiki/Brooklyn_Technical_High_School "Brooklyn Technical High School"), and [Junior's](/wiki/Junior%27s "Junior's").

## Transport operation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=DeKalb_Avenue&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Transport operation")]

Between Woodward Avenue and [Bushwick Avenue](/wiki/Bushwick_Avenue "Bushwick Avenue"), DeKalb Avenue is a two-way, two-lane street; between Bushwick Avenue and Fulton Street, it is one-way westbound. Lafayette Avenue is the corresponding parallel one-way street eastbound.

The [B38](/wiki/B38_(New_York_City_bus) "B38 (New York City bus)") bus, operated by [MTA New York City Transit](/wiki/New_York_City_Transit_Authority "New York City Transit Authority"), runs along the avenue west of Seneca Avenue. Ridgewood-bound buses run on Lafayette Avenue from Fulton Street to Broadway, then on Kossuth Place until Bushwick Avenue. The [B13](/wiki/B13_(New_York_City_bus) "B13 (New York City bus)") also stops on the corridor at Wyckoff Avenue, before deadheading back around via Saint Nicholas Avenue and Hart Street.

A bike lane, installed in 2004 and extended in 2008, exists between [Malcolm X Boulevard](/wiki/Utica_Avenue "Utica Avenue") and [Flatbush Avenue](/wiki/Flatbush_Avenue "Flatbush Avenue"). The [NYCDOT](/wiki/New_York_City_Department_of_Transportation "New York City Department of Transportation") proposed in 2008 to introduce [traffic calming](/wiki/Traffic_calming "Traffic calming") and other improvements to DeKalb Avenue.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Subway

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=DeKalb_Avenue&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Subway")]

Two [New York City Subway](/wiki/New_York_City_Subway "New York City Subway") stations serve DeKalb Avenue:

* [DeKalb Avenue](/wiki/DeKalb_Avenue_station_(BMT_lines) "DeKalb Avenue station (BMT lines)") ([B](/wiki/B_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "B (New York City Subway service)"), ​[D](/wiki/D_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "D (New York City Subway service)"), ​[N](/wiki/N_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "N (New York City Subway service)"), ​[Q](/wiki/Q_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "Q (New York City Subway service)"), ​[R](/wiki/R_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "R (New York City Subway service)"), and ​[W](/wiki/W_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "W (New York City Subway service)") trains) on the [BMT Fourth Avenue Line](/wiki/BMT_Fourth_Avenue_Line "BMT Fourth Avenue Line") and [BMT Brighton Line](/wiki/BMT_Brighton_Line "BMT Brighton Line")
* [DeKalb Avenue](/wiki/DeKalb_Avenue_station_(BMT_Canarsie_Line) "DeKalb Avenue station (BMT Canarsie Line)") ([L](/wiki/L_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "L (New York City Subway service)") train) on the [BMT Canarsie Line](/wiki/BMT_Canarsie_Line "BMT Canarsie Line")

In addition, the following subway stations are within one or two blocks of DeKalb Avenue:

* [Nevins Street](/wiki/Nevins_Street_(IRT_Eastern_Parkway_Line) "Nevins Street (IRT Eastern Parkway Line)") ([2](/wiki/2_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "2 (New York City Subway service)"), ​[3](/wiki/3_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "3 (New York City Subway service)"), ​[4](/wiki/4_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "4 (New York City Subway service)"), and ​[5](/wiki/5_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "5 (New York City Subway service)") trains) on the [IRT Eastern Parkway Line](/wiki/IRT_Eastern_Parkway_Line "IRT Eastern Parkway Line")
* [Lafayette Avenue](/wiki/Lafayette_Avenue_(IND_Fulton_Street_Line) "Lafayette Avenue (IND Fulton Street Line)") ([A](/wiki/A_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "A (New York City Subway service)") and ​[C](/wiki/C_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "C (New York City Subway service)") trains) on the [IND Fulton Street Line](/wiki/IND_Fulton_Street_Line "IND Fulton Street Line")
* [Fulton Street](/wiki/Fulton_Street_(IND_Crosstown_Line) "Fulton Street (IND Crosstown Line)"), [Clinton–Washington Avenues](/wiki/Clinton%E2%80%93Washington_Avenues_(IND_Crosstown_Line) "Clinton–Washington Avenues (IND Crosstown Line)"), [Classon Avenue](/wiki/Classon_Avenue_(IND_Crosstown_Line) "Classon Avenue (IND Crosstown Line)"), and [Bedford–Nostrand Avenues](/wiki/Bedford%E2%80%93Nostrand_Avenues_(IND_Crosstown_Line) "Bedford–Nostrand Avenues (IND Crosstown Line)") ([G](/wiki/G_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "G (New York City Subway service)") train) on the [IND Crosstown Line](/wiki/IND_Crosstown_Line "IND Crosstown Line")
* [Kosciuszko Street](/wiki/Kosciuszko_Street_(BMT_Jamaica_Line) "Kosciuszko Street (BMT Jamaica Line)") ([J](/wiki/J_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "J (New York City Subway service)") train) on the [BMT Jamaica Line](/wiki/BMT_Jamaica_Line "BMT Jamaica Line")
* [Central Avenue](/wiki/Central_Avenue_(BMT_Myrtle_Avenue_Line) "Central Avenue (BMT Myrtle Avenue Line)") ([M](/wiki/M_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "M (New York City Subway service)") train) on the [BMT Myrtle Avenue Line](/wiki/BMT_Myrtle_Avenue_Line "BMT Myrtle Avenue Line")
